title: Terminal Mental Models, Help, and Evidence · Spiral Review
summary: Investigate an unfamiliar Linux workstation without guessing, changing host state, or treating copied commands as explanations. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish terminal, shell, command, process, kernel, and operating-system responsibilities in an observed interaction.
Parse command names, options, operands, quoting, and exit status before predicting behavior.
Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — On a review host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence. Competency probe: Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.

Inspect the Terminal Evidence Record trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
